safety.secret_policy: Do not read or emit secret values. Only path, key-name, command, cwd, and existence metadata are allowed.
safety.destructive_actions: Never delete, prune, upgrade, or rewrite configs without an explicit operator-approved apply step.

fix_candidates.title: npm and npx exist inside nvm but are not on PATH
evidence: node resolves to /home/irfankabir/.local/bin/node
npm and npx are missing on PATH
/home/irfankabir/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/npm exists and reports 11.13.0
/home/irfankabir/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin/npx exists and reports 11.13.0
Cascade MCP configs and project instructions require npm/npx for TypeScript MCP servers

recommended_fix.strategy: Expose npm and npx consistently to non-interactive agents.
steps: Choose either PATH normalization or symlink normalization.
Preferred: add /home/irfankabir/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/bin to the agent-maintenance PATH used by systemd, hooks, and MCP configs.
Alternative: create user-level symlinks /home/irfankabir/.local/bin/npm and /home/irfankabir/.local/bin/npx pointing to the nvm binaries.
Run command -v npm and command -v npx in a non-interactive shell.
Run npm --version and npx --version.
Re-run MCP parity check.

fix_candidates.title: Canonical Cascade MCP config uses bare npx while current shell PATH lacks npx
evidence: /mnt/arch_data/home/caraxes/CascadeProjects/mcp_config.json has 25 servers
17 TypeScript MCP servers in that config use command npx
command -v npx failed in the audit environment
/home/irfankabir/.cursor/mcp.json and /home/irfankabir/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json use absolute npx paths and validate

recommended_fix.strategy: Make MCP command resolution deterministic across all clients.
steps: Pick a canonical command form: absolute npx path or controlled PATH.
Update the MCP generator/source of truth, not individual derived configs by hand.
Regenerate Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Copilot, Antigravity, and generic .mcp configs.
Validate every server command exists.
Validate every cwd exists after variable expansion.
Store parity report.

fix_candidates.title: Some MCP and automation configs still refer to stale /home/caraxes paths
evidence: Root AGENTS.md identifies /home/caraxes paths as stale
MCP parity scan found /home/caraxes references in archived and caraxes-side MCP configs
Earlier knowledge-loop scan also found /home/caraxes references

recommended_fix.strategy: Create a path-drift repair pass with allowlisted archived paths.
steps: Search for /home/caraxes across scripts and active configs.
Classify each occurrence as active, archive, fixture, or documentation.
Replace active occurrences with /home/irfankabir or /mnt/arch_data/home/caraxes as appropriate.
Add a CI or timer check that fails only active stale paths.
Document archived exceptions.

fix_candidates.title: hogsmade systemd user timers point to a missing driver path
evidence: hogsmade-harness.service ExecStart=%h/gruff/workspace/CascadeProjects/scripts/hogsmade-driver.sh
hogsmade-scan.service ExecStart=%h/gruff/workspace/CascadeProjects/scripts/hogsmade-driver.sh
/home/irfankabir/gruff/workspace/CascadeProjects/scripts/hogsmade-driver.sh does not exist

recommended_fix.strategy: Point the timers at the current CascadeProjects driver or disable them until the driver exists.
steps: Find the current intended hogsmade-driver.sh path under /home/irfankabir/CascadeProjects or /mnt/arch_data/home/caraxes/CascadeProjects.
If present, update ExecStart and CASCADE_WORKSPACE_ROOT together.
If absent, mark the two timers disabled in maintenance-authority.yaml and remove them from routine expectations.
Run systemd-analyze verify on the unit files.
Start each service manually once and inspect logs.

fix_candidates.title: Maintenance scripts and timers exist but no single maintenance authority file governs them
evidence: User timers exist for git hygiene, integrity monitor, network watchdog, node refresh, runtime health, stay-current, registry validation, security checks, and workspace maintenance
systemctl live state could not be queried in the sandbox
No single manifest maps timers to scripts, schedules, reports, safety mode, and owner

recommended_fix.strategy: Create a machine-readable maintenance authority registry and generate reports from it.
steps: Create agent-harness/registry/maintenance-authority.yaml.
Register each timer/service pair with schedule, ExecStart, report path, safety class, and expected outputs.
Add an offline unit-file validator.
Add a live-state validator for normal non-sandbox use.
Emit daily and weekly maintenance summaries into ~/.echoes and docs/agent-harness/reports.

fix_candidates.title: MCP server coverage differs across Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, Copilot, Antigravity, generic .mcp, and caraxes configs
evidence: Canonical current server set has 25 servers
/home/irfankabir/.cursor/mcp.json has all 25
/home/irfankabir/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json has all 25
/home/irfankabir/.copilot/mcp-config.json has 5
/home/irfankabir/.mcp.json has 1
Several caraxes-side user configs have 19-21 servers

recommended_fix.strategy: Decide intentional per-client MCP coverage and generate config variants from one source.
steps: Create mcp-authority.yaml with canonical 25-server registry.
Add per-client profiles: full, minimal, read-only, legacy, disabled.
Mark Copilot and generic .mcp as intentionally minimal or regenerate them.
Mark caraxes-side configs as archive mirrors or active clients.
Run parity check after every generator run.

fix_candidates.title: Go, Rust, Cargo, and Code resolve through snap and fail snap-confine checks
evidence: cargo, rustc, go, and code are present under /snap/bin
Version checks fail with snap-confine/AppArmor confinement error

recommended_fix.strategy: Either repair snapd/AppArmor or install non-snap developer toolchains and prefer them on PATH.
steps: Classify this as OS/toolchain degraded, not project failure.
Choose operator path: repair snap confinement or replace with apt/rustup/go tarball/deb packages.
Update tool-preflight to report degraded commands.
Do not run Go/Rust verification until this is fixed or bypassed by a non-snap toolchain.

fix_candidates.title: Active project scan found Node and Python projects without lockfiles
evidence: Active roots produced 337 manifest records across 173 project directories
52 Node projects have package.json without a detected lockfile
16 Python projects have pyproject.toml without uv.lock or poetry.lock
5 Dockerfile surfaces lack a nearby compose file

recommended_fix.strategy: Create per-ecosystem lockfile policy instead of blindly generating lockfiles everywhere.
steps: Classify each package.json as root app, workspace package, MCP server, fixture, archive, or vendored app.
For root apps and independently installed MCP servers, require package-lock.json unless npm workspace root owns the lock.
For Python active apps, require uv.lock unless intentionally library-only.
Add exceptions to dependency-policy.yaml.
Add a weekly lockfile-policy check.

fix_candidates.title: Some editor/agent config files need tolerant parsing or repair
evidence: /home/irfankabir/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json is 0 bytes
/home/irfankabir/.config/zed/settings.json is not strict JSON under Python json parsing
/mnt/arch_data/home/caraxes/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json exists and has content

recommended_fix.strategy: Use the right parser per client and repair empty active configs.
steps: Determine whether Zed settings are JSONC and parse with a JSONC parser.
If /home/irfankabir/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json is intended active, regenerate it from mcp-authority.yaml.
If it is intentionally empty, mark Antigravity MCP disabled in the client profile.
Add parser_kind to config-sources.yaml.

fix_candidates.title: Arch-era maintenance script overlaps with Ubuntu-era maintenance scripts
evidence: /home/irfankabir/scripts/toolchain-maintain.sh labels itself Arch Linux maintenance
Root AGENTS.md says current OS is Ubuntu 25.10 and pacman/brew/yay references are historical
/home/irfankabir/scripts/stay-current.sh is rewritten for Ubuntu 25.10

recommended_fix.strategy: Deprecate or guard Arch-era scripts so agents do not execute the wrong maintenance path.
steps: Add a top-of-file guard to toolchain-maintain.sh that exits on non-Arch systems unless --force-legacy is passed.
Register stay-current.sh as the Ubuntu package/toolchain report path.
Add script classification to maintenance-authority.yaml.
Search scripts for pacman/yay/brew and mark historical references.

fix_candidates.title: Timer/service unit files exist, but live status could not be verified from the sandbox
evidence: systemctl list-timers failed with bus access error in sandbox
Unit files are present under /home/irfankabir/.config/systemd/user
Offline ExecStart path validation found two missing hogsmade paths

recommended_fix.strategy: Maintain both offline and live validators.
steps: Offline validator: parse unit files, verify ExecStart and script arguments exist.
Live validator: run systemctl --user list-timers and systemctl --user status outside sandbox.
Persist both reports.
Fail maintenance health only when live mode is available and reports failed units.
